headline: Bobby Witt Jr. Goes First Overall, Ohtani Falls to Pick 2, and the Benchwarmers May Have Just Won the Whole Thing
subheadline: The 2026 MillerLite® BeerLeagueBaseball draft had everything: a shocking Ohtani slide, a defending champ loading up, and at least three teams who should just start planning their 2027 strategies now.
body:
# The 2026 MillerLite® BeerLeagueBaseball Draft Review
### *Your definitive, occasionally unhinged, always correct guide to what happened*

---

## **I. The War Room Opens**

You know that scene in *Moneyball* where Billy Beane is on the phone wheeling and dealing while everyone else is reacting in real time? That's what a 14-team fantasy draft feels like from the outside — a lot of confident people making decisions that will either look genius in October or get screenshotted and dunked on in the group chat by July. The 2026 MillerLite® BeerLeagueBaseball draft had all the drama you could want: a No. 1 overall pick that will be debated for weeks, Shohei Ohtani sliding to Pick 2 in a move that had at least six managers audibly gasping, and a defending champion in **Sugar Land Skeeters** who clearly came in with a plan and executed it like they had the answers written on their palm.

The steals in this draft were real and they were spectacular. **Canberra Wombats** pulling **Yordan Alvarez** at Pick 16 in Round 2 is the kind of move that makes you want to send a fruit basket. **Benchwarmers** getting **Paul Skenes** at Pick 15 in Round 2 — *the fifteenth pick of the entire draft* — is the fantasy equivalent of finding a $100 bill in an old jacket. Meanwhile, some teams in the middle rounds were reaching for guys like they were operating without an internet connection (more on that in Section IV, and yes, it's going to sting).

The biggest subplot going in: could **North Correa** finally get out of its own way? Back-to-back finishes of 14th and 12th. That's not a rebuild, that's a *hostage situation*. And the **Athletic Park Dragons** went from 6th to dead last in 2025, which means their owner showed up today with the desperate energy of someone who just got eliminated from *The Bachelor* and is writing an apology letter. Let's get into it.

---

## **II. Draft Order & Early-Pick Breakdown**

The top of this draft was genuinely fascinating because the conventional wisdom — Ohtani first overall, no debate, see you later — got completely torched the moment **North Correa** selected **Bobby Witt Jr.** at Pick 1. Now, look. Witt Jr. hit .290/.340/.489 with 33 HR, 117 RBI, 31 SB in 2025. He's an absolute monster and arguably the most complete fantasy player alive right now when you factor in category coverage across this league's 12-cat format. Did I think someone would actually pull the trigger on him over Ohtani? No. Is it defensible? Completely. But it handed **Athletic Park Dragons** the gift of Ohtani at Pick 2, which is like watching someone at an auction pass on the Mona Lisa and then getting it yourself for whatever the reserve bid was.

The rest of the first round was relatively chalk — **Judge** at 4, **José Ramírez** at 6, **Lindor** at 10 — but there were two picks that raised eyebrows. **Hiroshima Radiation** taking **Juan Soto** at 7 is excellent value given his move to the Mets and what he does for OBP in this format. And **Riverside Roughnecks** at Pick 12 landing **Jackson Chourio** over, say, some of the arms that were still available, tells you something about how they're building. First rounds set the floor. Middle rounds build the ceiling. And we've got a lot of ceilings to discuss.

---

## **III. The Draft Grades: All 14 Teams**

**NORTH CORREA** | Pick #1 | Grade: **C+**
*"We've been down so long, everything looks like up to me"*

Taking **Bobby Witt Jr.** first overall is a legitimate choice — the man is a five-category contributor and the best pure shortstop in the game. But after two straight basement finishes, the pressure was on to nail this entire draft, and the rest of it is... fine? **Pete Alonso** in Round 2 (who hit 34 HR with a .820 OPS in 2025) gives you the first base power, and **Logan Gilbert** in Round 3 is a quality arm. But **Alex Bregman** in Round 4 to a Cubs team is a real bet on a guy whose counting stats have declined. The pitching depth with **Sandy Alcantara** and **Spencer Strider** in Rounds 8 and 9 is high-upside/high-risk — both coming off injury. **Luis Robert Jr.** in Round 11 is the kind of name that makes you smile and then remember his 2025 injury history. This team has a very high ceiling if the injury gambles pay off, and a familiar floor if they don't. History suggests they'll land somewhere deeply mediocre and blame it on bad luck.

---

**ATHLETIC PARK DRAGONS** | Pick #2 | Grade: **B**
*"Fell from 6th to last in 2025 — the redemption arc starts here"*

Getting **Shohei Ohtani (Batter)** at Pick 2 is the kind of gift that makes a season. In 2025, Ohtani hit 44 HR, drove in 130 runs, stole 40 bases and hit .310. That's not a fantasy player, that's a fantasy *weapon of mass destruction*. **Matt Olson** in Round 2 (39 HR, 103 RBI in 2025) gives them legit power at first base. Then they go completely off the board and stack pitching through Rounds 3–7 with **Kyle Schwarber** (who's really a bat), **Michael King**, **Shota Imanaga**, **Aaron Nola**, and **Luis Castillo**. That's aggressive. Maybe too aggressive given the offensive holes it creates. **Ezequiel Tovar** in Round 10 for a guy hitting in Colorado is a dart throw I respect. The Dragons went last in 2025 and are clearly going all-in on a bounceback. Ohtani alone makes them dangerous. Everything else makes them *interesting*.

---

**WISCONSIN RAPIDS REAPERS** | Pick #3 | Grade: **B-**
*"Mookie at 3 is fine, but who's closing games for this team?"*

**Mookie Betts** at Pick 3 gives you the multi-position eligibility (2B/SS/OF) that is pure gold in a 12-cat league. **Ketel Marte** in Round 2 stacks that 2B slot beautifully. **William Contreras** in Round 3 is a legitimate catcher option. The problem? Zero proven closers. Their entire bullpen is **Justin Martinez**, **Jeff Hoffman**, and **Jason Foley** — a trio whose collective 2025 NSVH contributions were, to put it diplomatically, *aspirational*. Stacking hitters is fun until you get murdered in ERA and WHIP every week. **Randy Arozarena** in Round 9 after his move to Seattle is an interesting buy-low. **Merrill Kelly** in Round 12 is a veteran presence. One prior finish on record (10th in 2025) and this roster suggests they're building toward relevance rather than titles.

---

**ABBOTSFORD-COLBY TWINS** | Pick #4 | Grade: **A-**
*"The defending champ who lost a step in 2025 is hunting blood again"*

Former champion. Finished 6th last year after winning it all in 2024. This is a team that *knows how to draft*. **Aaron Judge** at 4 — coming off another monster year — anchors everything. **Jazz Chisholm Jr.** in Round 2 gives you 3B/2B/OF flexibility and the speed and power combo this format rewards. **Austin Riley** in Round 3. **Pablo López** in Round 4. This is methodical, smart drafting from a roster architect who's been here before. **Spencer Schwellenbach** in Round 6 is a genuine steal for a guy who looks like a future ace. **Xavier Edwards** in Round 9 for steals is a savvy late pick. The weakness is the catcher spot with **Logan O'Hoppe** feeling like a Round 12 consolation prize. But champion DNA is real and this team has it. Don't sleep on the Twins.

---

**BASEBALLPRO77** | Pick #5 | Grade: **B+**
*"Two-time champ. Still has the receipts. Elly is the guy."*

The most accomplished team in this league — won in 2023 and 2024, finished 7th in 2025 (the hangover year, classic). **Elly De La Cruz** at 5 is electric — 35 HR, 67 SB in 2025, absurd — but the speed matters enormously in a NSVH-heavy format. **Jackson Merrill** in Round 2 is a sneaky pick for a guy who quietly hit .280 with 25 HR. **Cole Ragans** in Round 3 is a legit ace in waiting. **Emmanuel Clase** in Round 4 is an elite closer and a steal at that spot. **Freddy Peralta** moving to the Mets in Round 6 is an intriguing dart. The late rounds with **Hunter Greene** and **Luis Arraez** show range. This team was built by someone who's done this before and doesn't panic. If they get their bounce-back year from **Royce Lewis** and **Bo Bichette**, watch out.

---

**ALBUQUERQUE BOMBERS** | Pick #6 | Grade: **A-**
*"Third place last year, quiet assassins this year"*

**José Ramírez** at 6 is an absolute cornerstone — hit .282 with 39 HR and 90 RBI in 2025 while playing elite defense and being generally wonderful. **Freddie Freeman** in Round 2 is still elite. **Garrett Crochet** in Round 3 going to Boston is the kind of arm that wins ERA categories. **Logan Webb** in Round 5 is durability and quality. The real steal here is **Colton Cowser** in Round 10 — a guy who broke out in 2025 and could be a top-50 value at that draft position. **Jasson Domínguez** in Round 11 is the high-variance breakout pick every contender needs. The Bombers finished 3rd last year and drafted like a team that wants to go one better. They might.

---

**HIROSHIMA RADIATION** | Pick #7 | Grade: **B**
*"The classic boom-or-bust roster: 2nd, 11th, 5th — pick a lane, man"*

**Juan Soto** at 7 is quality — .288/.419/.519 in 2025, and the OBP category alone makes him worth top-10 value. **Trea Turner** in Round 2 gives you speed. But then **Blake Snell** in Round 3 is the riskiest swing in the entire draft — a guy coming off a 2025 season that was more soap opera than baseball. **Tyler Glasnow** in Round 5 is another health bet. This team is essentially two catastrophic injuries away from going 11th again. If both arms stay healthy, this roster is *terrifying*. **Jose Altuve** in Round 4 for a guy whose 2025 numbers suggest the decline is real is a minor concern. **Jhoan Duran** going to Philly in Round 11 is good save upside. High variance, high ceiling, high blood pressure.

---

**WONDER WHARF WONDERDOGS** | Pick #8 | Grade: **A**
*"Perennial contender. 4th, 5th, 2nd. They are RIGHT THERE."*

The Wonderdogs are the Buffalo Bills of this league — phenomenally consistent, somehow haven't won. That ends this year, maybe. **Vladimir Guerrero Jr.** at 8 is elite. **Bryce Harper** in Round 2 is elite. **Corbin Burnes** in Round 3 is ace-level. **Framber Valdez** in Round 4 to Detroit. **Ronald Acuña Jr.** in Round 5 — if healthy — is maybe the steal of the entire draft at that position. That's a Round 5 pick for a guy who, when right, might be the best fantasy player on the board. **Devin Williams** moving to the Mets in Round 7 is an elite closer. **George Kirby** in Round 10. This roster is *loaded*. The Wonderdogs are finally going to win this thing and I'm saying it now.

---

**BUTTVILLE SKIDMARKS** | Pick #9 | Grade: **B-**
*"Three straight middling finishes. This draft is more of the same."*

**Kyle Tucker** to the Dodgers at 9 — good hitter, great situation. **Zack Wheeler** in Round 2 is a legitimate ace who posted sub-3 ERA in 2025. **Rafael Devers** in Round 3 moving to SF is a power bat. But then **Jacob deGrom** in Round 5? This is the pick that haunts you. deGrom is essentially fantasy mythology at this point — you're drafting the *idea* of Jacob deGrom more than the actual player. **Corey Seager** in Round 4 is quality if healthy (always that asterisk). The **Nolan Arenado** in Round 15 feels like nostalgia drafting. Solid floor, limited ceiling.

---

**GENERAL HOSPITAL** | Pick #10 | Grade: **C**
*"Back-to-back 13th place finishes. The name is doing a lot of heavy lifting here."*

**Francisco Lindor** at 10 is legitimately excellent — 33 HR, 98 RBI, 27 SB in 2025, quietly one of the best fantasy shortstops alive. But then *General Hospital* proceeds to do General Hospital things. **Chris Sale** in Round 3 at age 36 coming off a 2025 season where he somehow won the Cy Young — do you trust the encore? **Mike Trout** in Round 6 feels like picking a legendary car that hasn't started since 2022. Trout is a sentimental choice, not a strategic one. The pitching stack — **Bailey Ober**, **Bryan Woo** — is fine without being exciting. The path from 13th to relevance requires more than Lindor alone. Two straight last-place-adjacent finishes earn you no benefit of the doubt here.

---

**SUGAR LAND SKEETERS** | Pick #11 | Grade: **A-**
*"Defending champs know how to play this game"*

**Fernando Tatis Jr.** at 11 — coming off a healthy 2025 where he hit .278 with 33 HR and 28 SB — is excellent value at that slot. Then immediately grabbing **Tarik Skubal** in Round 2 (who was arguably the best pitcher in baseball in 2025 with a 2.51 ERA) is exactly what a defending champion does. **Yoshinobu Yamamoto** in Round 4. **Ryan Helsley** in Round 7 for saves. This team looks like it was drafted by someone who actually did their homework the night before instead of just winging it. The weakness is outfield depth and catcher (**Salvador Perez** is a great bat but fading defensively). **Jurickson Profar** in Round 11 after his 2025 breakout is interesting. Skeeters went from 13th to 3rd to 1st in three years. Don't tell me that's an accident.

---

**RIVERSIDE ROUGHNECKS** | Pick #12 | Grade: **B+**
*"Three straight middle-of-the-pack finishes. The breakout is coming."*

**Jackson Chourio** at 12 is a young star ascending. **Gunnar Henderson** in Round 2 — 35 HR, 30 SB ceiling, elite defender — might be the best Round 2 pick in the entire draft. **Ozzie Albies** in Round 3 gives you 2B counting stats. **Mason Miller** in Round 6 is an elite closer flying a bit under the radar. **Roki Sasaki** in Round 7 is the gamble I love — if he figures it out in LA, this is a Round 7 steal for an ace. **James Wood** in Round 5 is a future superstar at what should still be a discount ADP. The Roughnecks have quietly put together one of the deeper rosters in this draft and three consecutive disappointing finishes feel like they're due. This is the breakout year.

---

**CANBERRA WOMBATS** | Pick #13 | Grade: **B+**
*"3rd, 12th, 4th — they know what they're doing, they just need to stay healthy"*

**Corbin Carroll** at 13 is fine, but the real story is **Yordan Alvarez** falling to Pick 16 in Round 2 — that's a top-5 bat being handed to you at a bargain. **Wyatt Langford** in Round 3 is a future star. **Josh Hader** in Round 5 is elite closer value. **Cal Raleigh** in Round 6 is the best catcher available at that point. **Felix Bautista** in Round 9 returning from injury is high-risk/high-reward. The Wombats draft smartly and finish top-5 regularly. The 2024 collapse (12th) is the only blot. **Kodai Senga** in Round 12 coming back from injury is another recovery bet. Consistently competitive, occasionally brilliant.

---

**BENCHWARMERS** | Pick #14 | Grade: **A**
*"The snake draft's dirty secret: last pick in Round 1 means FIRST pick in Round 2"*

Here's the thing about picking 14th in a snake draft that most people forget until it happens to them — you get back-to-back picks. **Julio Rodríguez** at 14 (30 HR, 90 RBI, 25 SB projected trajectory), immediately followed by **Paul Skenes** at 15? That's a Round 2 pick for the most electric young arm in baseball, a guy who posted a 1.96 ERA in his 2025 debut and looked like he was sent from the future. Then **Oneil Cruz** in Round 3 for pure power/speed upside at SS. **Dylan Cease** in Round 4 for a quality arm. **Edwin Díaz** in Round 5 for elite saves. **Adley Rutschman** in Round 6 is the best catcher in fantasy. This roster is *stacked* and the Benchwarmers — who went 14th, 7th, 9th — look like they've figured something out. Paul Skenes at Pick 15 might be the single best pick of the entire draft.

---

## **IV. Draft Day Heroes & Villains**

**THE THREE BEST PICKS:**

**1. Paul Skenes, Round 2, Pick 15 — Benchwarmers (STEAL)**
A 2025 debut ERA of 1.96, 30 strikeouts per game, generational stuff — and he fell to *Pick 15*? The Benchwarmers are going to be telling this story for years. This is the "I got Hamilton tickets for $40" of fantasy baseball picks.

**2. Yordan Alvarez, Round 2, Pick 16 — Canberra Wombats (STEAL)**
One of the five best hitters in baseball falling to Pick 16 because everyone was hoarding shortstops and pitching in Round 1. The Wombats saw it and pounced. Classic patient drafting from a team with top-5 DNA.

**3. Tarik Skubal, Round 2, Pick 18 — Sugar Land Skeeters (STEAL)**
The defending champs grabbing the best pitcher in baseball in Round 2. Skubal's 2025 — sub-2.60 ERA, 220+ strikeouts, elite WHIP — is the kind of arm that wins pitching categories by itself. Getting him at 18 is the move of a champion.

**THE THREE WORST REACHES:**

**1. Jacob deGrom, Round 5, Pick 65 — Buttville Skidmarks (REACH)**
I respect the commitment to mythology but deGrom has pitched approximately 40 innings in the last three years combined. Drafting him in Round 5 over healthy, productive arms available is like buying a Lamborghini that's been in three accidents and currently has no engine. The upside is enormous. The probability is not.

**2. Mike Trout, Round 6, Pick 75 — General Hospital (REACH)**
This is the pick of a team that doesn't trust the process. Trout at Round 6 in 2026, after multiple injury-decimated seasons, is nostalgia over analytics. A team with back-to-back 13th place finishes cannot afford to spend a Round 6 pick on vibes.

**3. Nolan Arenado, Round 15, Pick 205 — Buttville Skidmarks**
This is probably fine as a 15th round flier but Arenado moving to Arizona (or wherever) at age 34 with declining power numbers gives me pause. It's the Round 15 equivalent of drafting a player because you liked him in 2019. Let it go.

---

## **V. Five Bold Predictions for 2026**

**1. The Benchwarmers win the whole thing.** Paul Skenes goes 18-5 with a 2.15 ERA, Julio Rodríguez hits 33 home runs, and the snake-draft back-to-back picks turn into the most talked-about draft story of the season. This team was built for 2026.

**2. General Hospital finishes 13th or worse for the third straight year.** Mike Trout misses 80 games, Chris Sale regresses, and Francisco Lindor — as good as he is — can't carry an entire roster. The hospital metaphor becomes uncomfortably accurate.

**3. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs finally break through and win the title.** Or they finish second again and the group chat becomes a crime scene. One or the other.

**4. Jacob deGrom pitches fewer than 60 innings for Buttville Skidmarks** and the Skidmarks finish below .500 in the standings specifically because of the Round 5 investment in a ghost. I will accept apologies in October.

**5. Riverside Roughnecks make their first-ever top-5 finish** behind the Chourio/Henderson/Sasaki core. Three years of 9th-11th finishes ends here. The breakout was always coming. This draft built the foundation.

---

## **VI. The Pick: Champion Crowned**

Look, I've been doing this long enough to know that the team you pick in March rarely wins in October and the team that wins in October was usually the one everyone forgot about by Memorial Day. But if I have to commit — and I do, because that's literally the point of this entire exercise — I'm riding with the **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs**.

Here's why. Vladimir Guerrero Jr. at Pick 8 is a franchise anchor. Bryce Harper at Pick 21 is a top-15 bat in this format. Corbin Burnes is a legitimate ace. Ronald Acuña Jr. at Pick 64 in Round 5 — *Round 5* — is the kind of value pick that decides seasons if he's healthy. Devin Williams for saves. George Kirby for ratio stability. This roster has no obvious holes, multiple elite players, and pitching depth that doesn't require them to win every coin flip.

More importantly: this team has been *knocking on the door* for three straight years. 4th, 5th, 2nd. That's not a team that's fading — that's a team that's been building toward something. They know how to draft for this specific league. They know the format. They've been in close battles and learned from them.

The Benchwarmers are the sexy pick and Skenes is a generational talent. The Abbotsford-Colby Twins have championship DNA. But the **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** feel like the team that's been waiting in the parking lot of this championship for three years, engine running, refusing to leave.

This is their year. And if it's not — if they finish second for the fourth time — then I will personally write the saddest eulogy in the history of fantasy baseball recaps and we will all gather and say goodbye to the dream together.

*Same time next year, everyone. Don't forget to hydrate.*

---
*MillerLite® BeerLeagueBaseball — Drink Responsibly, Draft Irresponsibly*
